text: I remember couple of years back I used to attend nearly all events in Nrb, every Friday and Saturday I had to be out somewhere. Even when I didn't have money I somehow wld find myself inside, I wld sometimes take alcohol from outside then go to club and buy one beer. Couple of years later, I can now afford attending any social space in Nrb, but I have become too choosy, I don't want to spend time in spaces full of strangers who you will never meet again, I have redefined fun. Now I love linking out with friends and overlanding, going camping, Mt climbing, traveling etc Club's are suddenly too loud for me, I care about my safety too, fights in clubs where pple throw bottles and glasses at each other honestly scare me, peace is priceless to me now.

text: Eddie,most men,especially those who settled down and had families decided to waste their tender youth in clubs and women . They've now hit their prime with nothing and there is a rush to catch up with the men who sacrificed the night life to build healthy relationships and wealth.Unfortunately, some blame their spouses for the stagnation and mistreat them while some have come to the realisation that they need to EVOLVE and rebuild.It is our hope that they'll thrive and do better.To the men who got it right from the start and accepted their mistakes along the way,keep going.Yes,nothing good comes from sexual immorality, drunkenness, wastage and narcissism.Men must reclaim their honour as God-given and do right by God and society.God help our sons.
